evals.prompt: how's the deal looking with Acme Corp?
evals.expected_output: Should look for extractions at ~/.agents/sales/acme-corp/extractions/. Should read all extraction files for the account. Should score all 6 dimensions (Problem, Impact, Urgency, Approach, People, Process) using the Strong/Emerging/Weak/Unknown scale. Should include direct buyer quotes as evidence. Should produce the full output format: scorecard table, What's Strong, What's Missing, Contradictions and Risk, Focus for Next Call.
assertions: Reads all extraction files from ~/.agents/sales/acme-corp/extractions/
Scores all 6 dimensions (Problem, Impact, Urgency, Approach, People, Process)
Uses the 4-level scale (Strong/Emerging/Weak/Unknown)
Includes direct buyer quotes as evidence for scored dimensions
Produces scorecard table in the specified format
Includes 'What's Strong' section with expanded evidence
Includes 'What's Missing' section with specific gaps
Includes 'Focus for Next Call' with actionable questions
Does NOT store the review as a file — outputs in conversation only

evals.prompt: run a deal review for GlobalPay — and show me the MEDDPICC view too
evals.expected_output: Should produce the standard 6-dimension review first, then map the same evidence to MEDDPICC categories (Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Identified Pain, Champion, Competition). Should not invent new evidence for the MEDDPICC view — same data, reorganized.
assertions: Produces standard 6-dimension scorecard first
Follows with MEDDPICC framework overlay
Maps dimensions correctly (e.g., Metrics → Impact, Identified Pain → Problem)
Does not invent new evidence for the framework view
Uses the same quotes in both views

evals.prompt: score this deal for Meridian Health — I think we're in good shape, they loved the demo
evals.expected_output: Should produce an honest assessment regardless of the user's optimism. If evidence is thin, dimensions should be scored Unknown or Weak — not inflated because the user feels good about it. Should specifically check whether 'they loved the demo' is backed by buyer quotes or is seller-side assertion. Should surface any gaps even if the user thinks the deal is strong.
assertions: Does not inflate scores based on user's stated optimism
Scores dimensions as Unknown when no evidence exists in extractions
Evaluates 'loved the demo' against actual buyer quotes — flags if only seller assertion
Surfaces gaps honestly even though user said 'we're in good shape'
Distinguishes between buyer enthusiasm (strong) and seller enthusiasm (weak)

evals.prompt: where do we stand with Acme? we only had one discovery call so far
evals.expected_output: Should still produce a review even with limited data. Should score most dimensions as Unknown or Emerging given only one call. Should not over-interpret limited evidence. The 'Focus for Next Call' section should be especially valuable here — surfacing what needs to be explored in call 2.
assertions: Produces a review even with only one extraction
Scores most dimensions as Unknown or Emerging appropriately
Does not overstate evidence strength from a single call
Focus for Next Call section provides specific questions for the next conversation
Notes that evidence will strengthen with additional calls

evals.prompt: what are we missing on the Acme deal? also can you check if they passed the specificity rating?
evals.expected_output: Should focus the review on What's Missing and explicitly run the specificity rating on the problem description. Should rate the problem description 1-5 on uniqueness — could this description only apply to Acme, or could it describe any company?
assertions: Emphasizes the What's Missing section per user's focus
Explicitly runs the specificity rating and rates 1-5
Evaluates whether the problem description is unique to this account
Provides specific gaps with actionable next steps

evals.prompt: can you process this transcript from my call with Acme today? [transcript text]
evals.expected_output: Should NOT process a transcript — should redirect to the sales-transcript-extractor skill. The deal review skill reads from existing extractions, it does not process raw transcripts.
assertions: Does NOT process the transcript
Redirects to sales-transcript-extractor skill by name
Explains that deal review reads from existing extractions

evals.prompt: draft a follow-up email for the Acme deal based on where we stand
evals.expected_output: Should NOT write a follow-up email. Should redirect and explain that artifact generation (emails, business cases, proposals) is outside deal review scope. Should offer to run the deal review so the user has the evidence base to inform a follow-up.
assertions: Does NOT generate a follow-up email
Explains artifact generation is outside this skill's scope
Offers to run a deal review instead
